summary: The Docker/local-PHP-based reproduction confirms that bare unserialize() on a crafted serialized payload instantiates the EGroupware\Api\Etemplate\Request\Files class, whose __destruct() method writes an attacker-controlled proof string to an attacker-chosen file path via file_put_contents(). This validates the PHP Object Injection vulnerability at addressbook_groupdav.inc.php:939.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause unserialize() to instantiate a POP gadget object whose destructor writes the proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF_OF_RCE_SUCCESS to a file on disk, demonstrating arbitrary file write capability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh executed the exploit script which (1) loaded the EGroupware autoloader from the target repository, (2) set the Files class static directory to a writable temp path, (3) called unserialize() on a crafted serialized Files object payload, (4) the __destruct() fired on object cleanup and wrote the proof string to /tmp/evohunt_test/evohunt_unserialize_proof.txt, and (5) the verification confirmed the proof file exists and contains the expected proof string.
verification_method: The exploit script checks that (a) unserialize() returns a valid object of class EGroupware\Api\Etemplate\Request\Files, (b) the proof file is created at the expected path, and (c) the file content contains the exact proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF_OF_RCE_SUCCESS. The script exits with code 0 on success and non-zero on failure, and run.sh propagates this exit code.
evidence_produced_by_run: unserialize() returned a valid object of class EGroupware\Api\Etemplate\Request\Files, confirming no class restrictions on the deserialization sink.
The proof file /tmp/evohunt_test/evohunt_unserialize_proof.txt was created by Files::__destruct() via file_put_contents(), confirming the POP gadget chain works end-to-end.

The file content a:2:{i:0;s:28:"EVOHUNT_PROOF_OF_RCE_SUCCESS";s:9:"destroyed";N;} contains the exact attacker-controlled proof string, confirming that attacker-controlled data flows through the entire deserialization chain into persistent file output.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub on the host (or falls back to the local repo checkout), then either builds a Docker image and mounts the checkout into the container at /target-repo, or runs the exploit directly with local PHP.
